summary: High‑energy Spring ’88 set with stellar China>Rider and tight jams
blurb: The show bursts with fire, highlighted by a ripping China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, a soaring To Lay Me Down, and a powerful Ballad Of A Thin Man. Jerry’s guitar is on the brink of brilliance while Brent’s keyboards soar through Half‑Step and The Other One. Overall a must‑listen for fans of the band’s late‑80s peak.
review:
April 1, 1988 at Meadowlands Arena captured one of the Grateful Dead’s most exhilarating spring performances. The first set kicks off with Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and quickly builds momentum through a fiery Jack Straw into a soaring To Lay Me Down that segues into an emphatic Ballad Of A Thin Man. Highlights keep rolling: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider opens the second set with classic energy, followed by a tight Estimated Prophet into an expansive Eyes Of The World. The rhythm section drives a crisp Drums‑Space transition into an otherworldly Other One that stretches into a deep, exploratory jam before the band wraps up with Wharf Rat, Throwing Stones and a rousing Not Fade Away, closing with a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore.

Across all available sources the performance is praised for its consistency and excitement. Jerry’s solos on Deal, To Lay Me Down and the China Cat sequence are described as “fiery” and “roasting,” while Brent’s B‑3 work shines on Half‑Step and The Other One. Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming shows continual improvement, especially in the post‑drums space jam that many call a major highlight. Even with a few weaker moments—such as a fluffed encore and an uneven Push Comes To Shove—the show remains a standout example of the band’s late‑80s vitality. The best listening experience comes from the Miller soundboard capture (gd1988-04-01.sbd.miller.91160.sbeok.flac16), which offers crystal‑clear, well‑balanced audio that lets every instrument shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across eight of eleven recordings, with repeated praise for the China>Rider segue, To Lay Me Down, Ballad Of A Thin Man, and The Other One. Multiple reviewers call the show 'high‑energy', 'fire' and 'stellar', highlighting Jerry’s fiery solos and Brent’s soaring keyboards. No consistent negative comments were found, just a few mixed notes about specific songs, so a robust positive adjustment is justified.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source praised for high‑quality, clear, well‑balanced audio with no dropouts; captures the band’s dynamics and highlights (China>Rider, Ballad Of A Thin Man) best of all available mixes.

key_highlights: Ripping first set that builds from Half‑Step to a soaring To Lay Me Down
Classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue ignites the second set
Exploratory Other One jam fueled by intense post‑drums space segment
Brent’s keyboard work shines on Half‑Step and The Other One, while Jerry delivers fiery solos throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers fiery, razor‑sharp solos on Deal, To Lay Me Down and China Cat; vocals are raspy yet emotionally resonant, earning praise as 'brilliant' throughout the night.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid vocal presence on Jack Straw and adds hype with the ‘Jersey fillies’ chant, contributing to the set’s energy.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work is consistently praised—soaring lines on Half‑Step, killer fills on The Other One, and overall 'killer' performance that lifts the jam sections.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming shows continual improvement; tight, driving rhythms underpin the set and his post‑drums space jam is highlighted as a major high point.
